name: supabase
reason: Core backend for Lifeflow - Supabase handles auth, real-time, storage, and PostgreSQL for user wellness data and payroll records.

name: supabase-postgres-best-practices
reason: Colombian payroll schemas require complex PostgreSQL design; this skill ensures Row-Level Security, indexing, and query optimization are production-grade.

name: vercel-react-native-skills
reason: Lifeflow is a React Native/Expo app - this skill provides architecture patterns, performance optimization, and best practices for the primary client platform.

name: react-native-best-practices
reason: Callstack is the React Native authority; their skill covers navigation, state management, and native module patterns essential for Expo/RN production apps.

name: expo/building-native-ui
reason: Expo-specific UI patterns for the biometrics dashboard, wellness tracking UI, and gamified components that define Lifeflow's user experience.

name: stripe-best-practices
reason: Stripe/RevenueCat payment integration is a core requirement; official Stripe skill covers subscriptions, webhooks, Colombian peso handling, and compliance.

name: stripe-integration
reason: Complements stripe-best-practices with implementation patterns for Stripe Connect, subscription tiers, and webhook idempotency for the wellness SaaS model.

name: expo-deployment
reason: EAS Build and Submit configuration for App Store/Play Store releases; critical for Lifeflow's CI/CD pipeline to Colombian and international markets.

name: expo-cicd-workflows
reason: Automates EAS builds on PR merge with GitHub Actions, ensuring continuous delivery for both staging and production Expo apps.

name: deploy-to-vercel
reason: Deploys the Node.js/Express API and any Next.js admin panel to Vercel with preview environments for each PR.

name: claude-api
reason: Lifeflow's AI mentor feature is built on Claude; this skill covers prompt caching, tool use, streaming, and Anthropic SDK best practices for production AI features.

name: nodejs-backend-patterns
reason: Node.js/Express API patterns for the payroll calculation engine, biometric data ingestion, and REST endpoints consumed by the Expo app.

name: auth-implementation-patterns
reason: JWT + biometric auth + Supabase RLS patterns needed for secure access to wellness data and Colombian payroll records with GDPR/local compliance.

name: nextjs-supabase-auth
reason: Supabase auth with server-side rendering patterns; useful for any Next.js admin dashboard complementing the mobile Expo app.

name: database-migration
reason: Colombian payroll compliance requires frequent schema changes as regulations evolve; safe migration patterns prevent production data loss.

name: prisma-database-setup
reason: Type-safe ORM for the payroll calculation engine; Prisma + Supabase Postgres gives full TypeScript coverage over SISBEN, PILA, and DIAN data models.

name: react-native-architecture
reason: Scalable folder structure, offline-first patterns, and state management architecture for biometric sync, RevenueCat integration, and AI mentor sessions.

name: github-actions-templates
reason: Pre-built CI/CD workflows for Expo EAS builds, Supabase migrations, and Vercel API deployments - core infrastructure for the Lifeflow release pipeline.

name: test-driven-development
reason: TDD discipline is critical for payroll calculation logic where correctness is legally required; ensures Colombian DIAN/UGPP compliance via automated tests.

name: frontend-design
reason: Wellness app UX requires expert design craft; this top-3 global skill (325K installs) provides component architecture, accessibility, and visual hierarchy for the Lifeflow dashboard.
